title: Fresh clone doesn't carry refs/jjf/*: new collaborator sees empty planner
body:
# Symptom

Fresh `git clone` (or `jj git clone --colocate`) of a jjforge
project does NOT bring `refs/jjf/*` along. A new collaborator
joining a project sees an empty planner until they manually
run `git fetch origin 'refs/jjf/*:refs/jjf/*'`.

# Repro

Against any pushed jjforge repo:

```
$ jj git clone --colocate central.git bob
$ cd bob
$ git for-each-ref refs/jjf
# (empty)
$ jjf ls
jjf: the `issues` bookmark does not exist; run `jjf init` first
```

Same result with plain `git clone`.

# Root cause

Git's default fetch refspec is `refs/heads/*:refs/remotes/...`
and `jj`'s clone doesn't extend that to `refs/jjf/*`. The
`refs/jjf/*` namespace requires explicit configuration on the
local clone before round-trip works.

# Fix candidates

1. New verb `jjf clone <url> <dir>` that wraps git clone and
   writes the refspec into `.git/config`.
2. `jjf init`, when run on a fresh clone of a repo with a
   matching remote, writes the refspec into `.git/config` and
   `git fetch`es.
3. `jjf pull origin` adds the refspec to config on first run
   (lazy auto-config).

(2) is probably the smallest change and matches the user's
mental model — they already run `jjf init` once per repo.

# Cross-links

This is one of three siblings sharing a root-cause cluster
around `refs/jjf/meta/*` and the multi-machine UX. See also:
- F-002 (`jjf pull` exits 1 from fresh clone)
- F-003 (`jjf push` exits 1 from fresh clone, persistently)
- F-010 (`meta/format-version` isn't validated — likely
  should be deleted from the protocol, which would also fix
  F-002/F-003 cleanly)

# Confidence

Reproducible 100%. Tested in
`scratchpad/qa/r04-sync/` (alice → central → bob).
